title: Class II HLA loci accumulate non-coding substitutions at 9.8–17.9× the coding rate (DPB1 peak); DRB1 non-coding identity against reference is only 40.6%

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Class II HLA loci accumulate non-coding substitutions at 9.8–17.9× the coding ra'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: 4-field IPD-IMGT/HLA alleles for the three Class I (HLA-A/B/C) and three Class II (HLA-DRB1/DQB1/DPB1) loci. Coding vs non-coding regions are partitioned per the IPD-IMGT/HLA GFF3 annotations.
Per-locus *01:01:01:01 reference allele used as the divergence baseline. All other alleles are compared per-bp to this reference in both coding and non-coding regions.

operations.note: NC/coding fold = (mean non-coding substitution %) / (mean coding substitution %) per locus.
Isolate DRB1 non-coding identity against reference. 1 − 0.594 = 0.406 (40.6% identity).

statistics.name: per_locus_mean_noncoding_substitution_rate_pct
statistics.note: Per-bp non-coding substitution rate against *01:01:01:01 reference, averaged across alleles. Class I: 13.3–24.1%. Class II: 40.5–59.4%.

statistics.name: per_locus_mean_coding_substitution_rate_pct
statistics.note: Per-bp coding substitution rate. Class II DPB1 is notably low at 2.3% — the class minimum.

statistics.name: per_locus_nc_over_coding_fold
statistics.note: NC/coding fold ratio. Class I range 2.7–5.6×. Class II range 9.8–17.9×. DPB1's 17.9× is the extremum and reflects the 2.3% coding / 40.5% non-coding combination.

statistics.name: class_i_min_nc_over_coding_fold
statistics.note: HLA-C is the Class I minimum.

statistics.name: class_ii_min_nc_over_coding_fold
statistics.note: HLA-DQB1 is the Class II minimum — still 2× the Class I maximum (5.6).

statistics.name: drb1_nc_identity_vs_reference_fraction
statistics.note: 1 − 0.594 non-coding substitution rate = 0.406 identity. Alleles share barely half their DRB1 non-coding sequence (≈ 12.4 kb of intron).

inference.justification: Three conjuncts: (1) Class II minimum fold ratio (9.8×) exceeds Class I minimum (2.7×) — stat-vs-stat — establishes the Class I / Class II contrast; (2) Class II min > 8× ensures the gap is substantive rather than marginal (Class I max is 5.6×, so 8× is the conservative separator); (3) DRB1 non-coding identity < 0.5 — alleles share less than half their DRB1 non-coding sequence against the reference, which is the headline 'extreme intronic divergence' claim.
inference.failure_mode: If any Class II fold ratio fell into the Class I range (<6×), the 'Class II is extreme' claim softens to 'Class II is somewhat elevated'. If DRB1 non-coding identity rose above 0.5, the 'alleles share more than half their introns' framing would require rewriting.

conclusion.assertion: Across the six HLA loci, Class I non-coding substitution rates are 2.7–5.6× the coding rate, while Class II loci show 9.8–17.9× — a qualitative break. HLA-DPB1 is the extremum at 17.9× (2.3% coding vs 40.5% non-coding). HLA-DRB1 alleles share only 40.6% non-coding identity against the *01:01:01:01 reference, meaning ~12.4 kb of intronic sequence differs across the allelic landscape. The disparity between coding and non-coding rates at Class II is incompatible with uniform neutral mutation and indicates either strong purifying selection on coding, active non-coding diversification under balancing selection, or both.

scope.scale_note: Per-locus aggregate claim. Per-region (UTR / intron 1 / intron 2 / …) decomposition exists in the paper's Table 8 but is a separate child fixture.

confidence.note: Per-locus means over allele populations; no inferential p-value pinned — this is a descriptive substitution-rate claim.

external_assumptions.statement: *01:01:01:01 is a valid divergence baseline at each locus; alternative references would produce similar fold ratios modulo mild shifts.
Coding / non-coding partitioning follows the IPD-IMGT/HLA GFF3 annotations; alternative partitions (e.g., excluding UTRs from non-coding) would shift the rates by at most a few percentage points.

The observed NC/coding disparity at Class II reflects genuine evolutionary-rate heterogeneity across the locus, not a GC or repeat-density artifact — the same ratios hold after masking repeat-rich intronic regions in a sensitivity analysis (noted in the manuscript Methods).
